summary: Ukrainian drones struck the machine hall of the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant, causing a through explosion but no radiation rise. Ukraine received an additional IRIS‑T air‑defence launcher as Russian drone and missile attacks intensified, with over 2,300 drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 rockets reported in a week. Multiple tactical UAV sorties were observed across northern, eastern and southern fronts, targeting fuel depots, oil refineries and logistics corridors.
key_points: Ukrainian drone attack damaged the machine block building at Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant, with normal radiation levels reported (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558).
Ukraine received a new IRIS‑T surface‑to‑air missile system amid reports of 2,300 drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 rockets launched by Russia in the past week (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
A fuel tanker convoy was ambushed by a UAV in Sal'kove, Kherson region, forcing the rest of the fuel column to turn back, preventing fuel delivery to Crimea (Source: @eRadarrua/94476).
Ukrainian drones were observed over Dnipro, targeting the NTZ plant and a UAV over Yavornytskyi (Source: @eRadarrua/94447).
Russian authorities reported a UAV strike on the machine hall of Zaporizhzhia NPP, prompting IAEA inspection (Source: @tass_agency/378327).
Multiple Shahed (kamikaze) attacks were reported on Korosten, Rivne, Sarni and other locations in the north and east (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67310, @mon1tor_ua/67315, @mon1tor_ua/67303).
Ukrainian long‑range drones claimed capability of up to 3,500 km range, according to the Main Intelligence Directorate (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30553).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian drones struck the machine hall of the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant, causing structural damage but no radiation increase. Ukraine bolstered its air defence with an IRIS‑T launcher as Russian drone, bomb and missile attacks surged, with over 2,300 UAVs reported in a single week. UAV activity continued across multiple fronts, targeting fuel depots, oil refineries and logistics routes, complicating Russian supply lines.

points: A Ukrainian drone hit the machine block building at Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant, producing a through explosion; Rosatom reported normal radiation background (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558).

Ukraine announced acquisition of an IRIS‑T surface‑to‑air missile system, emphasizing its importance for protecting civilians against the high volume of Russian drones, guided bombs and rockets (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
Ukrainian forces claimed long‑range drone capability up to 3,500 km, as stated by the head of the long‑range drone division of the Main Intelligence Directorate (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30553).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over Dnipro, including two over the city and one over the NTZ plant, indicating continued reconnaissance and strike activity (Source: @eRadarrua/94447).

Multiple Shahed kamikaze attacks were reported in the north: two on Rivne and its surroundings, one on Sarni, and two on Korosten, highlighting persistent low‑altitude threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67310, @mon1tor_ua/67315, @mon1tor_ua/67303).
Russian officials confirmed a UAV strike on the Zaporizhzhia NPP machine hall and noted that IAEA experts are inspecting the site (Source: @tass_agency/378327).
Russia reported that over the past week Russian forces launched more than 2,300 drones, nearly 1,560 guided aviation bombs and 108 rockets against Ukrainian targets (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
Russian authorities announced temporary restrictions on imports of certain berries and vegetables from Armenia, reflecting broader economic measures (Source: @tass_agency/378323).
Russia’s foreign ministry highlighted a threat of ballistic weapons from the north, though no specific deployment was detailed (Source: @kpszsu/63350).
Russian media quoted Maria Zakharova dismissing U.S. claims about space theft, indicating ongoing information warfare (Source: @tass_agency/378318).
Ukrainian drones were reported moving from Zhytomyr through Kharkiv toward Zaporizhzhia, indicating eastward strike paths (Source: @kpszsu/63353).
Shahed attacks were logged near Korosten and Rivne, both situated on the eastern side of the northern front, showing continued pressure on logistics hubs (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67310).
Ukrainian intelligence noted that frontline logistics and the southern land corridor face constant drone attacks up to 200 km deep, hampering supply lines (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30559).
Russian forces in Konstantinovka continued artillery and aviation superiority, with the city experiencing extensive destruction (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30559).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over the NTZ plant in Dnipro, a strategic industrial target in the east (Source: @eRadarrua/94447).
Ukrainian tactical UAVs were reported over Chernihiv and northern Kyiv regions along the Belarus border, moving westward (Source: @kpszsu/63401).
Shahed attacks were recorded on Rivne and its environs, with two drones reported on 31 May (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67303).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted over Sumy near Buryn and Putyvl from the east, suggesting pressure on northern Sumy oblast (Source: @kpszsu/63394).
A UAV strike on a school in Enerhodar caused damage to windows and a bus, indicating the spread of aerial threats to northern Black Sea coastal areas (Source: @tass_agency/378330).
Ukrainian drones were observed over Zhytomyr heading toward Malyn, moving southward along the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/63372).
A fuel tanker convoy was attacked by a UAV in Sal'kove, Kherson region, forcing the remainder of the fuel column to reverse and halting fuel delivery to Crimea (Source: @eRadarrua/94476).
Ukrainian drones were reported moving from Zhytomyr through Kherson toward Zaporizhzhia, indicating south‑westward strike vectors (Source: @kpszsu/63353).
Ukrainian intelligence warned that frontline logistics and the southern land corridor are under constant drone attacks, affecting supply depth up to 200 km (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30559).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over the Dnipro region, including a northbound flight over Nikopol district (Source: @kpszsu/63397).
Russian forces continued raids on oil refineries and fuel depots in the south, as noted in the broader assessment of the conflict (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30559).
Ukrainian drones struck the Zaporizhzhia NPP machine hall, marking a significant aerial strike on a nuclear facility (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558).
Russia reported launching over 2,300 drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 rockets in a single week, indicating high intensity of aerial attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
U.S. tested a low‑cost anti‑drone system (MADIS) based on JLTVs with 30‑mm guns and Stinger missiles, reflecting efforts to counter costly drone interceptions (Source: @tass_agency/378317).
Shahed kamikaze drones were reported hitting multiple locations in the north and east, including Rivne, Korosten and Sarni (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67310, @mon1tor_ua/67315).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over Dnipro, NTZ plant and near Yavornytskyi, showing continued reconnaissance and strike capability (Source: @eRadarrua/94447).

key_developments: Ukrainian drones damaged the machine block building at Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant, with radiation levels remaining normal (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30558).
Ukraine received an IRIS‑T air‑defence launcher as Russian drone, bomb and missile attacks surged, with over 2,300 drones reported in a week (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
A fuel tanker convoy was ambushed by a UAV in Sal'kove, Kherson, forcing the remainder to turn back and halting fuel delivery to Crimea (Source: @eRadarrua/94476).
Russian authorities confirmed a UAV strike on the Zaporizhzhia NPP machine hall and noted IAEA inspection activities (Source: @tass_agency/378327).
Multiple Shahed kamikaze attacks were recorded on Rivne, Korosten and Sarni, indicating persistent low‑altitude threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67310, @mon1tor_ua/67315, @mon1tor_ua/67303).
Ukrainian intelligence claimed drone range capability up to 3,500 km, expanding potential strike reach (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30553).
U.S. tested a cost‑effective anti‑drone system (MADIS) using JLTV platforms with 30‑mm guns and Stinger missiles to counter expensive drone interceptions (Source: @tass_agency/378317).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV operations have demonstrated the ability to strike high‑value infrastructure, including the Zaporizhzhia nuclear facility, while Russian aerial attacks remain numerically dominant, with thousands of drones and missiles deployed weekly. The emergence of long‑range Ukrainian drones expands the threat envelope, potentially reaching deep into Russian‑held territories. Shahed kamikaze attacks continue to pressure northern and eastern logistics nodes, complicating Russian supply lines and civilian protection measures.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is reinforcing its air defence with systems such as IRIS‑T and seeking low‑cost counter‑UAV solutions, reflecting a shift toward layered protection. International observers, including the IAEA, are monitoring the nuclear plant incident, while NATO allies are discussing increased support amid heightened drone activity. Russian authorities are emphasizing the severity of UAV strikes and are implementing legal measures to involve private security in counter‑drone operations.
sections.intelligence_notes: The reported 3,500 km drone range suggests potential for strikes far beyond current frontlines, necessitating expanded radar coverage. Fuel logistics in the south are vulnerable, as evidenced by the Sal'kove tanker ambush, indicating a need for hardened supply routes. The high volume of Russian aerial munitions underscores the importance of rapid, cost‑effective interception technologies, such as the MADIS system under test.
